summary: Spotless Maven Plugin is normally run inside Maven, and its artifact omits Maven-provided compileOnly APIs that tests need when exercising Mojo/configuration classes directly.

deterministic_setup.reason: Spotless Mojo classes extend Maven plugin API types that are compileOnly/provided by Maven, not exposed by the resolved artifact.
Spotless configuration and Mojo code reference MavenProject and related Maven runtime types used when exercising plugin behavior outside Maven.
The plugin references Eclipse Aether repository/session types as compileOnly Maven runtime inputs.
Mojo classes carry Maven plugin annotations that are compileOnly in the upstream build and useful for annotation/reflection coverage.

agent_guidance: Exercise the plugin as a Maven plugin/configuration library against a temporary project with real files. Prefer built-in generic steps such as trimTrailingWhitespace, endWithNewline, replace/replaceRegex, or licenseHeader so coverage does not depend on external formatter downloads, npm, or a service. If invoking Mojo classes directly, initialize the temporary base/build directories and required Maven-style fields/components before calling execute.
risks: Many advanced Spotless formatters provision external artifacts or tools; avoid making the core test depend on network-installed formatters.
Direct Mojo execution without Maven container injection can fail unless required fields are initialized explicitly.
